description: Cryptographic primitives, protocols, and security foundations.

facts.context: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) block size is:
distractors: 256 bits matching the largest key size available
64 bits like the older DES algorithm uses
variable depending on the mode of operation

facts.context: RSA encryption security relies on hardness of:
distractors: the discrete logarithm problem exclusively
finding hash collisions in SHA-256
solving systems of linear equations

facts.context: Diffie-Hellman key exchange without authentication is vulnerable to:
distractors: passive eavesdropping alone without active interference
only quantum computers with sufficient qubits
brute force attacks on the shared secret

facts.context: ECDSA (Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm) uses curves over:
distractors: real numbers with floating point arithmetic
complex numbers for all curve operations
rational numbers with arbitrary precision

facts.context: Zero-knowledge proofs allow a prover to demonstrate:
facts.truth: statement truth without revealing the witness
distractors: nothing at all to the verifier about the claim
that the statement is actually false not true
the complete witness to everyone observing

facts.context: A cryptographic hash function collision resistance means:
facts.truth: finding two inputs with same output is hard
distractors: every input produces a unique output always guaranteed
the output length equals the input length exactly
reversing the hash to find input is impossible

facts.context: CBC mode requires the initialization vector (IV) to be:
distractors: secret and never transmitted with the ciphertext
the same fixed value for all encryptions
derived deterministically from the plaintext

facts.truth: past sessions safe even if long-term key compromised
distractors: future sessions are protected from current attacks
all keys are rotated every 24 hours automatically
encryption is unbreakable by any future technology

facts.truth: integrity and authenticity using shared secret
distractors: confidentiality of the message contents only
non-repudiation like digital signatures provide
compression of the message to fixed length

facts.truth: execution time variations leaking secret data
distractors: clock synchronization errors between systems
network latency to delay key exchange
timestamp manipulation in log files

facts.context: Key derivation functions (PBKDF2, Argon2) are slow by design to:
distractors: ensure compatibility with legacy hardware systems
provide time for network key synchronization
allow users to cancel key generation if needed

facts.truth: signatures from leaf to trusted root CA
distractors: only the end-entity certificate signature itself
certificate file format is valid JSON or XML
domain name matches the server IP address
